name: pymc
tagline: Guides Bayesian modeling in PyMC: model building, MCMC/NUTS sampling, diagnostics, and LOO/WAIC model comparison.
verdict: Clean, pure-documentation skill. No credential access, no network egress beyond a declared `uv pip install` for PyMC itself, no obfuscation, no hidden or manipulative instructions, no destructive operations — all code is standard Bayesian-modeling Python (pymc/arviz) that stays within the stated scope. Only minor polish gaps: no explicit 'when not to use' gu

highlights.summary: No section states when NOT to use this skill (e.g. non-Bayesian/frequentist tasks, other PPLs like Stan); a user could invoke it outside its comfort zone without guidance.

No explicit statement of the expected final deliverable/output format (e.g. saved idata file, report); output is implied only through the workflow steps and 'Save results with idata.to_netcdf' note (l
File is fairly dense with many near-duplicate code blocks (regression variants, sampling snippets repeated in both workflow and quick-reference sections), pushing it over typical context budget.
